tracks.meaning: a device or mechanism designed to catch or ensnare something, especially an animal
a trick or stratagem designed to catch someone out or deceive them
a situation or circumstance from which it is difficult or impossible to escape
a curved section of pipe that holds liquid to form a seal and prevent gases from passing back
a geological formation that stops the movement of oil or gas and allows it to accumulate
in soccer or basketball, to stop and control a moving ball with one's foot or body
an ambush or sudden attack laid for an enemy
the mouth or speech, often used dismissively as 'shut your trap'
a trapdoor in a theatre stage floor used for entrances and exits
a diagnostic routine that interrupts program execution when specified conditions occur
a program or code section designed to catch errors or unexpected inputs
a situation in card games or gambling where a player is forced into a disadvantageous position
